evals.prompt: Add a function that checks if a string is a valid email address
evals.expected: Agent writes a failing test for email validation BEFORE writing the implementation
assertions: A test file is created or modified before the implementation file
The test is run and shown to fail
Implementation code is written only after seeing the failure

evals.prompt: The test expects isEmail('user@example.com') to return true. Make it pass.
evals.expected: Agent writes the simplest correct implementation, not a full regex library
assertions: Implementation handles the specific test case
No unnecessary complexity or edge case handling beyond what tests require

evals.prompt: Users report that login fails when their email has a plus sign like user+tag@gmail.com
evals.expected: Agent writes a reproducing test FIRST, then fixes the bug
assertions: A test case for plus-sign emails is written first
Test is run and fails (reproducing the bug)
Fix is applied and test passes

evals.prompt: Write a test for the parse_config function
evals.expected: Agent detects Cargo.toml and uses cargo test
assertions: Uses cargo test to run the test
Test is in a #[test] function or #[cfg(test)] module

evals.prompt: Write a test for the formatDate utility function
evals.expected: Agent uses vitest when test_runner is set to vitest
assertions: Uses npx vitest run to execute
Test uses describe/it or test() syntax

evals.prompt: Add a test for the calculateTotal function and make it pass
evals.expected: Agent does not auto-commit when commit_at_green is false
assertions: No git commit is made automatically
